FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Regional bank M&A kicks off with First Hawaiian's $2B TriCo deal, while big banks face mixed signals: strong Q2 earnings ahead but potential July rate hike looming.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian acquiring TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks Hawaiian's return to mainland after BNP Paribas exit.
Q2 earnings set to boom from SpaceX IPO fees and geopolitical volatility trading, with commercial lending pickup providing tailwind.
Fed rate-hike odds for July rising on oil-price spike from Hormuz tension—direct NIM compression risk for deposit-rich franchises.
JPMorgan leadership: Erdoes retires after board names Petno and Rohrbaugh as co-presidents; Dimon frames as 'natural change.'

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Wealth management consolidation hits peak intensity: CalPERS lands Russell Investments while RIA platforms race to scale; legal headwinds emerge for fintech.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments ($580B pension buying multi-asset manager) in play that lets Russell shed debt and adopt non-profit governance—the Vanguard reputational playbook.
RIA consolidation fever: Hightower posts organic growth highs, Choreo closes 4th acquisition post-SpaceX win, ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for standalone platform, and Wealthfront crosses $100B AUM.
Carson Group restructures go-to-market into separate W-2 and independent channels to sharpen focus and organic growth while Summitry ($4B RIA) undergoes leadership transition under Aspen ownership.
Betterment hit with class action over cash sweep accounts—alleged profit generation from uninvested client cash at partner banks without proper compensation.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: P&C industry celebrates decade-high underwriting profits amid geopolitical insurance headwinds and strategic consolidation in Lloyd's.

bullets.text: $84B underwriting gain over two years: U.S. P&C posts strongest profit in a decade with lowest combined ratio in 10 years, driven by pricing and underwriting discipline across $1T in direct premiums.
Hormuz tanker attacks escalate marine underwriting risk: Two UAE supertankers hit in strait handling 21% of global crude, likely spiking war-risk premiums and volatility for maritime P&L.
Saudi Re deepens Lloyd's footprint with £8.95M AdA Risk stake: Acquisition of 22.5% holding signals continued strategic consolidation in London market with regulatory approval in hand.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Mastercard explores Vocalink exit while regional fintech faces headwinds; CMBS distress signals servicer activity ahead.

bullets.text: Mastercard considering sale of Vocalink, the UK payments platform acquired in 2016—potential return to original banking owners signals strategic portfolio shift.
CMBS special servicing rate jumped in June 2026 on large retail, office, and lodging distress—expect increased workout activity in property-backed securitizations.
Nubank clears Mexican banking authorization; Klarna faces Dutch class action—regional expansion momentum offset by regulatory/litigation risks for fintech players.

PE & Deals.lede: Four stories on PE fundraising and deal flow, led by Arctos' novel $6.2B GP financing structure and mega-cap energy infrastructure play.

bullets.text: Arctos Partners closes $6.2B GP financing using 'self-liquidating' Keystone strategy—a debut structure in GP-financing that lets funds return capital to LPs while keeping portfolio exposure.
Blackstone, Apollo, KKR co-invest $5.34B in Williams' energy infrastructure projects, signaling robust large-cap PE appetite for power transition assets.
Moelis poaches Evercore VP and four-person team to build LP-led secondaries practice, capitalizing on market expansion as major sponsors source deals at favorable valuations.

Markets & Macro.lede: Bank earnings season opens amid record profitability, QT risks loom, and capital rotation accelerates beyond mega-cap tech.

bullets.text: Major bank earnings start Tuesday with sky-high expectations; market braced for potential disappointment after recent run-up.
U.S. banks hitting record profits on NIMs and cost cuts, but QE's structural impact on balance sheets makes QT riskier than policymakers realize—banks now depend on central bank liquidity.
Russell 2000 has best year since 2003 as investors rotate out of mega-cap tech into small caps on AI capex and valuation relief.
ECB tapping 36 banks including Deutsche and BPCE to pilot digital euro in 2025, recruiting both backers and skeptics to pressure-test the model.

Newsletters.lede: Big bank earnings dominate tomorrow's calendar, while ETF flows turn negative and portfolio tech tackles tax friction.

bullets.text: Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow—expect detailed reads on credit quality, deposits, and consumer health across lending and trading.
ETF flows swung negative in a rare week; redemptions hit equity, bond, and alternative products as investors reassess positioning.
Vanguard and Envestnet launched tools to reduce tax friction when RIAs convert client portfolios between model allocations—addressing real workflow pain.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Four stories spanning incentive misalignment in sports, computational complexity and competition theory, startup capital creativity in AI, and Apple's strategic weakness versus OpenAI.

bullets.text: NBA players gaming shooting percentages by taking end-of-quarter heaves instead of higher-value plays—classic principal-agent problem where individual metrics diverge from team outcomes.
Computational complexity determines market structure: if P = NP, firms can efficiently detect and sustain cartels; if P ≠ NP, collusion becomes undetectable and competition persists as equilibrium.
Groq's Jonathan Ross locked down $20B NVIDIA partnership via unconventional financing (Grok Bonds) after near-bankruptcy, proving creative capital solutions can unlock AI infrastructure deals.
Apple's OpenAI lawsuit over trade-secret theft masks the real problem: no coherent AI strategy and playing catch-up to better-positioned rivals.

all.lede: Regional bank M&A heats up as First Hawaiian snaps TriCo for $2B, while major bank earnings loom and QT risks linger.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian acquiring California's TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks First Hawaiian's return to mainland U.S. after BNP Paribas exit.
Big Six bank earnings reports start tomorrow; market watching closely for signals on credit quality, deposits, and consumer health across lending and trading.
Mastercard exploring sale of Vocalink UK payments platform, potentially returning it to original British bank owners after 2016 acquisition.
QE structurally rewired bank balance sheets and funding models, making quantitative tightening riskier than policymakers realize due to persistent central bank liquidity dependence.
